heading: Transportation policy
label: 13101 Transportation policy

children.label: A
children.text: to recognize and preserve the inherent advantage of each mode of transportation;

children.label: B
children.text: to promote safe, adequate, economical, and efficient transportation;

children.label: C
children.text: to encourage sound economic conditions in transportation, including sound economic conditions among carriers;

children.label: D
children.text: to encourage the establishment and maintenance of reasonable rates for transportation, without unreasonable discrimination or unfair or destructive competitive practices;

children.label: E
children.text: to cooperate with each State and the officials of each State on transportation matters; and

children.label: F
children.text: to encourage fair wages and working conditions in the transportation industry;

children.label: A
children.text: encourage fair competition, and reasonable rates for transportation by motor carriers of property;

children.label: B
children.text: promote efficiency in the motor carrier transportation system and to require fair and expeditious decisions when required;

children.label: C
children.text: meet the needs of shippers, receivers, passengers, and consumers;

children.label: D
children.text: allow a variety of quality and price options to meet changing market demands and the diverse requirements of the shipping and traveling public;

children.label: E
children.text: allow the most productive use of equipment and energy resources;

children.label: F
children.text: enable efficient and well-managed carriers to earn adequate profits, attract capital, and maintain fair wages and working conditions;

children.label: G
children.text: provide and maintain service to small communities and small shippers and intrastate bus services;

children.label: H
children.text: provide and maintain commuter bus operations;

children.label: I
children.text: improve and maintain a sound, safe, and competitive privately owned motor carrier system;

children.label: J
children.text: promote greater participation by minorities in the motor carrier system;

children.label: K
children.text: promote intermodal transportation;

children.label: A
children.text: to cooperate with the States on transportation matters for the purpose of encouraging the States to exercise intrastate regulatory jurisdiction in accordance with the objectives of this part;

children.label: B
children.text: to provide Federal procedures which ensure that intrastate regulation is exercised in accordance with this part; and

children.label: C
children.text: to ensure that Federal reform initiatives enacted by section 31138 and the Bus Regulatory Reform Act of 1982 are not nullified by State regulatory actions; and
in overseeing transportation by water carrier, to encourage and promote service and price competition in the noncontiguous domestic trade.

children.heading: .—Administration To Carry Out Policy
children.label: b .—Administration To Carry Out Policy
children.text: This part shall be administered and enforced to carry out the policy of this section and to promote the public interest.
